title: Clinical Psychologist
description: Clinical psychologists assess, diagnose, and treat mental, emotional, and behavioral disorders. They use psychotherapy, psychological testing, and other methods to help individuals, families, and groups improve their mental health and well-being.
content: Clinical psychologists in India work in hospitals, clinics, private practices, schools, and community mental health centers. They address a wide range of issues, including depression, anxiety, addiction, trauma, and relationship problems. The role requires strong empathy, communication skills, and a deep understanding of human behavior and cultural nuances.

financialFeasibility.note: Master's and MPhil/PhD degrees are typically required, which can be financially demanding. Government colleges offer lower fees, but competition is high. Private practice requires initial investment but offers higher earning potential.

sectorScore.remarks: Opportunities exist in both government and private sectors. Private practice and corporate wellness programs are growing areas. Government jobs offer stability, while private sector roles often provide higher salaries.

relatedCareers.reason: Provides therapy and counseling services to individuals and groups.
relatedCareers.title: Counseling Psychologist

relatedCareers.reason: Medical doctor specializing in mental health, can prescribe medication.
relatedCareers.title: Psychiatrist
courses: M.B.B.S - Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery

relatedCareers.title: Social Worker
